user_outcome: The sworn TUI board actually renders tracks and slices for any current-format release (the originally reported bug); the blocked-slice view resolves the real per-track worktree path and reads violations from proof.json instead of a stale, silently-wrong fallback.
rationale: internal/tui/board.go's LoadBoard hand-parses index.md frontmatter for a tracks: YAML key that sworn render's current output never emits (tracks moved to a Markdown table) — silently renders zero tracks, no error. internal/tui/blocked.go's LoadBlockedView has the same frontmatter parse for worktree_path (silently falls back to repoRoot instead of the real path) and its own ExtractViolations regex-scrapes proof.md sections instead of reading proof.json.not_delivered (already a clean string array). Both files migrate to internal/board's already-correct oracle/BoardRecord machinery. The existing test (tui_test.go TestBoardViewShowsSlices) hand-writes its own pre-migration-shape index.md fixture directly in the test source rather than generating one via sworn render — this is why the bug shipped undetected; fixtures must be regenerated from real render output.

effort_complexity.rationale: Two files' data-loading functions rewired to an already-correct reader, plus regenerating hand-authored test fixtures in tui_test.go to match real sworn render output. No new logic invented, but touches live BoardView/BlockedView struct shapes consumed elsewhere in the tui package.

acceptance_criteria.text: When `BoardView.LoadBoard` is called for a release with a committed `board.json`, it SHALL populate `Tracks` and `Slices` by reading `board.json` (via `internal/board`'s existing reader) instead of parsing `index.md` frontmatter.
When `LoadBlockedView` resolves a track's worktree path, it SHALL read it from `board.json` via the same reader, not from `index.md` frontmatter's `worktree_path` key.
When `LoadBlockedView`'s `ExtractViolations` needs a slice's not-delivered items, it SHALL read `proof.json`'s `not_delivered` array directly instead of regex-scraping `proof.md` sections.

`internal/tui/tui_test.go`'s `TestBoardViewShowsSlices` (and any sibling test using a hand-written `index.md` fixture with the legacy `tracks:` YAML shape) SHALL be regenerated to build its fixture via the real `sworn render`/`internal/board` render path, not a hand-authored string literal — closing the exact Reachability Gate gap that let this bug ship undetected.

Reachability artefact: with this fix applied, launching the TUI (`sworn` with no args or `sworn top`), selecting any release with a `board.json` (e.g. `2026-06-30-sworn-operational-readiness`), and pressing enter SHALL show its real tracks and slice states in the board pane — captured as a terminal screenshot or recorded session, not just a passing unit test.
Releases with no `board.json` (genuinely pre-migration) SHALL continue to render via the existing legacy fallback — this slice does not regress support for them.
After the change, `go build ./...` SHALL succeed and `go test ./internal/tui/...` SHALL pass.
